doc_title: HUD Tenant Rights Fact Sheet TRF-2026-IL
label: fabricated
reason: The document inverts Illinois law by claiming tenants must vacate within 5 days with no right to cure or court hearing, when in fact the Illinois Eviction Act gives tenants the right to pay and stay and requires a court eviction order befor
response_turn1:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 96
REASON: This document misstates Illinois law — under the Illinois Eviction Act (formerly Forcible Entry and Detainer Act), a 5-day notice gives tenants the right to cure by paying rent, and landlords must file an eviction lawsuit and obtain a court order before a tenant is required to vacate; the "obligation to vacate" framing is legally false and not how HUD or the Illinois AG would issue guidance.
response_turn2:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 96
REASON: The document inverts Illinois law by claiming tenants must vacate within 5 days with no right to cure or court hearing, when in fact the Illinois Eviction Act gives tenants the right to pay and stay and requires a court eviction order before removal.
